source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A rectangular piece of paper is wrapped around a cylinder. Then an angled straight cut is made through the points $\mathrm{X}$ and $\mathrm{Y}$ of the cylinder as shown on the left. The lower part of the piece of paper is then unrolled. Which of the following pictures could show the result?
<image1>
<image2>
extraction_notes: Options are only given as labels without textual option content, so the answer is kept as the original option letter.

issues: question_text contains two image placeholders ('<image1>' and '<image2>'), but only one top-level image path is provided.
top-level image_paths and metadata.image_paths are inconsistent.

image_dependency: Question asks which picture could show the unrolled result after a cut on a cylinder, so the visual diagram and option images are central to solving it.
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the sample is primarily visual rather than text-solvable.
The prompt references a shown cylinder and candidate pictures, implying spatial/shape information not fully recoverable from text alone.
multi_step: Solving requires interpreting the cut on the wrapped cylinder, mentally unrolling the paper, and then matching the resulting shape to one of the options.
This involves geometric/spatial transformation rather than a single direct lookup.
There are 5 choices, so the solver must compare the inferred unrolled pattern against multiple visual candidates.
verifiability: answer_type is choice/option and answer_text is a single explicit option ('C'), making the final output easy to check.
The underlying reasoning is geometry-based and in principle can be validated by checking whether the cut pattern on the cylinder unwraps to the selected picture.
registry_passed=true supports basic asset availability, though registry issues reduce confidence somewhat.

risk_flags: question_text contains two image placeholders ('<image1>' and '<image2>'), but only one top-level image path is provided.
top-level image_paths and metadata.image_paths are inconsistent.

decision_reasons: Question text, one image asset, and the answer are all present, so the sample still retains recoverable core semantics.
The problem is highly image-dependent, but the registry reports two image placeholders while only one top-level image path is provided, creating an important asset gap.
Inconsistent image path metadata lowers confidence in completeness and makes this a follow-up candidate rather than a clean keep.
Per high-recall collection policy, the sample should not be rejected because the core question-answer structure is still available.
